featured.title: Berlin Ghost Stations: The 15 Stops Trains Passed Without Stopping
featured.summary: For 28 years West Berlin trains ran through East Berlin without stopping, past 15 sealed and guarded platforms. Here is the full list, what happened at each one, and the only two places where it is still worth getting off today.
featured.alt: The empty tiled mezzanine level of Nordbahnhof S-Bahn station in Berlin
featured.label: Newest route-history story

miniPosts.title: Is Berlin Walkable? How Far Apart the Main Sights Really Are (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Is Berlin walkable? The central sights are far closer than the map suggests, and flat underfoot. Here is how far apart they really are, the one walk to do on foot, and when to take the train instead.
miniPosts.alt: The Bode Museum dome and the TV Tower rising across the Spree on Berlin's Museum Island, two of the central sights that sit within an easy walk of each other

miniPosts.title: Do You Need a Car in Berlin? Driving, Parking and Renting Explained (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Should you rent a car for Berlin? For almost every city trip the honest answer is no. Here is why the trains win, what driving actually costs, and when a car earns its place.
miniPosts.alt: A central Berlin street in Mitte with parked cars, bollards, tram wires and the TV Tower in the distance

miniPosts.title: Berlin Emergency Numbers: 112, 110 and What to Do in an Emergency (2026)
miniPosts.summary: Which number do you call in Berlin? 112 for an ambulance or fire, 110 for police. Here is the full list, plus the doctor line, night pharmacies and stolen wallets.
miniPosts.alt: A Berlin fire brigade ambulance marked with the 112 emergency number on a city street

miniPosts.title: Berlin Airports Explained: Is Tegel Still Open in 2026?
miniPosts.summary: Tegel, Schönefeld, BER: which one do you actually fly into? The calm answer is one airport for scheduled passenger flights. Here is what happened to the old ones, and how to get from BER into town.
miniPosts.alt: The broad entrance plaza and central facade of Berlin Brandenburg Airport Terminal 1

posts.title: Where to Watch the 2026 World Cup in Berlin (No, There's No Brandenburg Gate Fan Mile This Year)
posts.summary: The 2026 World Cup is in North America, and Berlin has no Brandenburg Gate fan mile this year. Here is where to actually watch: free screens, beer gardens and bars, with every kick-off in Berlin time.
posts.alt: A summer evening football public viewing in Berlin with a relaxed crowd, outdoor screen and TV Tower skyline

posts.title: How Many Days Do You Need in Berlin? An Honest Local's Answer (2026)
posts.summary: Berlin is big on the map but small where it counts. Here is how many days you actually need, what each extra day unlocks, and when a day trip is worth it.
posts.alt: Warm high-angle view over central Berlin with the TV Tower rising above Mitte and the River Spree

posts.title: Do You Need a Visa to Visit Berlin? Entry Rules, EES and ETIAS Explained (2026)
posts.summary: Do you need a visa, is there a new EU form, and will you get stuck at the border? For most passports the answer is short. Here are Berlin's 2026 entry rules, made calm.
posts.alt: The Brandenburg Gate in central Berlin, empty in the early morning light
